message.content:
Implement the following plan: # Make @Mentions Clickable in Comments & Opinions ## Context When users type `@username` via the mention autocomplete in comments, replies, and opinions, the text is stored as plain text (e.g. `"great post @loren-the-great!"`). Currently it renders as static text in a `

` tag with no interactivity. Users expect `@mentions` to be clickable links that navigate to the mentioned user's profile. ## Approach Create a shared `MentionText` component that parses plain text for `@username` patterns, rendering them as clickable styled spans. Clicking navigates to `/u/{username}` (or `/profile` if it's the current user's own username). ## Files to create ### 1. `client/src/components/mentions/MentionText.jsx` (new) - Accepts `text` prop (the raw comment/opinion string) - Regex-splits text on `@([\w-]+)` pattern - For each `@username` match, render a `` that on click: - If `username === currentUser.username` → `navigate('/profile')` - Else → `navigate('/u/{username}')` - Uses `useAuth()` for current user's username, `useNavigate()` for routing - Non-mention text segments render as plain text - Stops event propagation on click (parent containers have their own click handlers) ### 2. `client/src/components/mentions/MentionText.css` (new) - `.mention-link` — styled as clickable: color accent, cursor pointer, font-weight 500, hover underline ## Files to modify Replace raw `{text}` with `` in these 7 locations: | # | File | Line | Current | Change to | |---|------|------|---------|-----------| | 1 | `comments/commentsCards.jsx` | 188 | `{comments?.comment}` | `` | | 2 | `SidebarOpinions/SidebarOpinions.jsx` | 141 | `{opinionsData.opinion}` | `` | | 3 | `SidebarOpinions/visitedProfileOpinions.jsx` | 122 | `{opinion.opinion}` | `` | | 4 | `SidebarOpinions/OpinionsCardRepy.jsx` | 111 | `{opinion.opinion}` | `` | | 5 | `SidebarOpinions/opinionViewer.jsx` | 100 | `{opinion.opinion}` | `` | | 6 | `SidebarOpinions/MyOpinions.jsx` | 90 | `{opinion.opinion}` | `` | | 7 | `SidebarOpinions/OpinionssPage.jsx` | 276 | `{opinion.opinion}` | `` | Each file gets an `import MentionText from '../mentions/MentionText'` (path adjusted per location). ## Verification 1. Post a comment containing `@someuser` via the mention autocomplete 2. Confirm the `@someuser` text appears styled/colored differently from surrounding text 3. Click it → should navigate to `/u/someuser` 4. Type your own `@username` in a comment → click it → should navigate to `/profile` 5. Check opinions in sidebar, opinion viewer, visited profile opinions, and opinions page all render clickable mentions too 6. Confirm clicking a mention doesn't also trigger the parent container's click handler (e.g. opening opinion viewer) If you need specific details from before exiting plan mode (like exact code snippets, error messages, or content you generated), read the full transcript at: C:\Users\johnm\.claude\projects\C--Users-johnm-iskrib-v3\60908a73-13f2-4399-adcd-54b562cdfc6d.jsonl

planContent:
# Make @Mentions Clickable in Comments & Opinions ## Context When users type `@username` via the mention autocomplete in comments, replies, and opinions, the text is stored as plain text (e.g. `"great post @loren-the-great!"`). Currently it renders as static text in a `

` tag with no interactivity. Users expect `@mentions` to be clickable links that navigate to the mentioned user's profile. ## Approach Create a shared `MentionText` component that parses plain text for `@username` patterns, rendering them as clickable styled spans. Clicking navigates to `/u/{username}` (or `/profile` if it's the current user's own username). ## Files to create ### 1. `client/src/components/mentions/MentionText.jsx` (new) - Accepts `text` prop (the raw comment/opinion string) - Regex-splits text on `@([\w-]+)` pattern - For each `@username` match, render a `` that on click: - If `username === currentUser.username` → `navigate('/profile')` - Else → `navigate('/u/{username}')` - Uses `useAuth()` for current user's username, `useNavigate()` for routing - Non-mention text segments render as plain text - Stops event propagation on click (parent containers have their own click handlers) ### 2. `client/src/components/mentions/MentionText.css` (new) - `.mention-link` — styled as clickable: color accent, cursor pointer, font-weight 500, hover underline ## Files to modify Replace raw `{text}` with `` in these 7 locations: | # | File | Line | Current | Change to | |---|------|------|---------|-----------| | 1 | `comments/commentsCards.jsx` | 188 | `{comments?.comment}` | `` | | 2 | `SidebarOpinions/SidebarOpinions.jsx` | 141 | `{opinionsData.opinion}` | `` | | 3 | `SidebarOpinions/visitedProfileOpinions.jsx` | 122 | `{opinion.opinion}` | `` | | 4 | `SidebarOpinions/OpinionsCardRepy.jsx` | 111 | `{opinion.opinion}` | `` | | 5 | `SidebarOpinions/opinionViewer.jsx` | 100 | `{opinion.opinion}` | `` | | 6 | `SidebarOpinions/MyOpinions.jsx` | 90 | `{opinion.opinion}` | `` | | 7 | `SidebarOpinions/OpinionssPage.jsx` | 276 | `{opinion.opinion}` | `` | Each file gets an `import MentionText from '../mentions/MentionText'` (path adjusted per location). ## Verification 1. Post a comment containing `@someuser` via the mention autocomplete 2. Confirm the `@someuser` text appears styled/colored differently from surrounding text 3. Click it → should navigate to `/u/someuser` 4. Type your own `@username` in a comment → click it → should navigate to `/profile` 5. Check opinions in sidebar, opinion viewer, visited profile opinions, and opinions page all render clickable mentions too 6. Confirm clicking a mention doesn't also trigger the parent container's click handler (e.g. opening opinion viewer)

content.text:
All done. Here's a summary of what was created and modified:

**New files:**
- `client/src/components/mentions/MentionText.jsx` — Parses text for `@username` patterns, renders them as clickable styled spans. Clicking navigates to `/u/{username}` (or `/profile` for the current user). Stops event propagation to prevent parent click handlers from firing.
- `client/src/components/mentions/MentionText.css` — Accent-colored, cursor-pointer, font-weight 500, hover underline styling.

**Modified files (7)** — Added `MentionText` import and replaced raw text rendering with `<MentionText text={...} />`:
1. `comments/commentsCards.jsx` — `{comments?.comment}`
2. `SidebarOpinions/SidebarOpinions.jsx` — `{opinionsData.opinion}`
3. `SidebarOpinions/visitedProfileOpinions.jsx` — `{opinion.opinion}`
4. `SidebarOpinions/OpinionsCardRepy.jsx` — `{opinion.opinion}`
5. `SidebarOpinions/opinionViewer.jsx` — `{opinion.opinion}`
6. `SidebarOpinions/MyOpinions.jsx` — `{opinion.opinion}`
7. `SidebarOpinions/OpinionssPage.jsx` — `{opinion.opinion}`

content.thinking:
The problem is clear. Both `opinionViewer.jsx` and `OpinionsCardRepy.jsx` use `contentEditable` divs for reply input, not `<textarea>` elements. The `useTextareaMention` hook relies on `e.target.selectionStart` which only works on `<textarea>` and `<input>` elements, not `contentEditable` divs.

The solution is to convert these `contentEditable` divs to `<textarea>` elements (like commentsCards.jsx does) and wire up the mention hook + dropdown. Or create a version of the mention hook that works with contentEditable.


I'll follow the pattern from commentsCards.jsx and swap out the contentEditable divs for textareas, then integrate the mention system the same way.

content.text:
Found the problem. Both reply inputs (`opinionViewer.jsx` and `OpinionsCardRepy.jsx`) use `contentEditable` divs instead of `<textarea>` elements. The `useTextareaMention` hook relies on `e.target.selectionStart` which only works on `<textarea>`/`<input>`, not contentEditable.

The fix is to convert these to `<textarea>` (like commentsCards.jsx already does) and wire up the mention hook + dropdown. Let me write the plan.

lines: +# Add Mention Autocomplete to Opinion Reply Inputs

-When users type `@username` via the mention autocomplete in comments, replies, and opinions, the text is stored as plain text (e.g. `"great post @loren-the-great!"`). Currently it renders as static text in a `

` tag with no interactivity. Users expect `@mentions` to be clickable links that navigate to the mentioned user's profile.

+The opinion reply inputs in `opinionViewer.jsx` and `OpinionsCardRepy.jsx` use `contentEditable` divs. The `useTextareaMention` hook requires `<textarea>` (it reads `selectionStart`), so typing `@` shows no dropdown. The comment reply input in `commentsCards.jsx` works because it uses a `<textarea>`.

-Create a shared `MentionText` component that parses plain text for `@username` patterns, rendering them as clickable styled spans. Clicking navigates to `/u/{username}` (or `/profile` if it's the current user's own username).

+Convert the `contentEditable` reply divs to `<textarea>` elements and wire up `useTextareaMention` + `MentionDropdown`, matching the pattern already used in `commentsCards.jsx` and `OpinionsEditor.jsx`.
-- For each `@username` match, render a `<span className="mention-link">` that on click:
-- Uses `useAuth()` for current user's username, `useNavigate()` for routing
-- Non-mention text segments render as plain text
-- Stops event propagation on click (parent containers have their own click handlers)
-- `.mention-link` — styled as clickable: color accent, cursor pointer, font-weight 500, hover underline
-Replace raw `{text}` with `<MentionText text={text} />` in these 7 locations:
-Each file gets an `import MentionText from '../mentions/MentionText'` (path adjusted per location).
+- Remove the `textAreaRef.innerText` usage in `cancelTyping` and `handleSubmitReplyOpinion` — with a controlled `<textarea>`, just `setReplyOpinion('')` is sufficient
-1. Post a comment containing `@someuser` via the mention autocomplete
-2. Confirm the `@someuser` text appears styled/colored differently from surrounding text
-4. Type your own `@username` in a comment → click it → should navigate to `/profile`
-5. Check opinions in sidebar, opinion viewer, visited profile opinions, and opinions page all render clickable mentions too
-6. Confirm clicking a mention doesn't also trigger the parent container's click handler (e.g. opening opinion viewer)
+1. Open an opinion in the viewer → click Reply → type `@lor` → dropdown should appear with matching users
+2. Select a user from dropdown → `@username ` inserted into textarea
+3. Submit the reply → text with `@mention` saved correctly
+4. Open a nested reply (OpinionsCardRepy) → same behavior
+5. Existing MentionText rendering still makes the `@mention` clickable after submission

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Make @Mentions Clickable in Comments & Opinions ## Context When users type `@username` via the mention autocomplete in comments, replies, and opinions, the text is stored as plain text (e.g. `"great post @loren-the-great!"`). Currently it renders as static text in a `

` tag with no interactivity. Users expect `@mentions` to be clickable links that navigate to the mentioned user's profile. ## Approach Create a shared `MentionText` component that parses plain text for `@username` patterns, rendering them as clickable styled spans. Clicking navigates to `/u/{username}` (or `/profile` if it's the current user's own username). ## Files to create ### 1. `client/src/components/mentions/MentionText.jsx` (new) - Accepts `text` prop (the raw comment/opinion string) - Regex-splits text on `@([\w-]+)` pattern - For each `@username` match, render a `` that on click: - If `username === currentUser.username` → `navigate('/profile')` - Else → `navigate('/u/{username}')` - Uses `useAuth()` for current user's username, `useNavigate()` for routing - Non-mention text segments render as plain text - Stops event propagation on click (parent containers have their own click handlers) ### 2. `client/src/components/mentions/MentionText.css` (new) - `.mention-link` — styled as clickable: color accent, cursor pointer, font-weight 500, hover underline ## Files to modify Replace raw `{text}` with `` in these 7 locations: | # | File | Line | Current | Change to | |---|------|------|---------|-----------| | 1 | `comments/commentsCards.jsx` | 188 | `{comments?.comment}` | `` | | 2 | `SidebarOpinions/SidebarOpinions.jsx` | 141 | `{opinionsData.opinion}` | `` | | 3 | `SidebarOpinions/visitedProfileOpinions.jsx` | 122 | `{opinion.opinion}` | `` | | 4 | `SidebarOpinions/OpinionsCardRepy.jsx` | 111 | `{opinion.opinion}` | `` | | 5 | `SidebarOpinions/opinionViewer.jsx` | 100 | `{opinion.opinion}` | `` | | 6 | `SidebarOpinions/MyOpinions.jsx` | 90 | `{opinion.opinion}` | `` | | 7 | `SidebarOpinions/OpinionssPage.jsx` | 276 | `{opinion.opinion}` | `` | Each file gets an `import MentionText from '../mentions/MentionText'` (path adjusted per location). ## Verification 1. Post a comment containing `@someuser` via the mention autocomplete 2. Confirm the `@someuser` text appears styled/colored differently from surrounding text 3. Click it → should navigate to `/u/someuser` 4. Type your own `@username` in a comment → click it → should navigate to `/profile` 5. Check opinions in sidebar, opinion viewer, visited profile opinions, and opinions page all render clickable mentions too 6. Confirm clicking a mention doesn't also trigger the parent container's click handler (e.g. opening opinion viewer)
